nodes.description: Patient-derived fibroblasts and cells expressing SLC25A24 Arg217 variants show abnormal mitochondrial morphology, reduced ATP-linked oxygen consumption, lower mitochondrial matrix ATP, and membrane-potential hyperpolarization.

evidence.reference_title: De Novo Mutations in SLC25A24 Cause a Disorder Characterized by Early Aging, Bone Dysplasia, Characteristic Face, and Early Demise.
evidence.snippet: showed altered mitochondrial morphology, a decreased proliferation rate, increased mitochondrial membrane potential, and decreased ATP-linked mitochondrial oxygen consumption.

evidence.reference_title: De Novo Mutations in SLC25A24 Cause a Craniosynostosis Syndrome with Hypertrichosis, Progeroid Appearance, and Mitochondrial Dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: ATP content in the mitochondrial matrix was lower than that in control cells.

nodes.description: SLC25A24-mutant cells develop mitochondrial swelling, especially after hydrogen peroxide exposure, indicating abnormal response to oxidative stress.

evidence.snippet: the probands' cells showed mitochondrial swelling, which was exacerbated upon treatment with hydrogen peroxide (H2O2).

evidence.reference_title: De Novo Mutations in SLC25A24 Cause a Craniosynostosis Syndrome with Hypertrichosis, Progeroid Appearance, and Mitochondrial Dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: mitochondrial dysfunction with increased sensitivity to oxidative stress is due to the SLC25A24 mutations.

evidence.snippet: SLC25A24 | solute carrier family 25 member 24 | hgnc:20662 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: De Novo Mutations in SLC25A24 Cause a Craniosynostosis Syndrome with Hypertrichosis, Progeroid Appearance, and Mitochondrial Dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: we identified the recurrent de novo mutations c.650G>A (p.Arg217His) and c.649C>T (p.Arg217Cys) in SLC25A24 in five unrelated girls

evidence.reference_title: De Novo Mutations in SLC25A24 Cause a Disorder Characterized by Early Aging, Bone Dysplasia, Characteristic Face, and Early Demise.
evidence.snippet: all carried the de novo missense variant c.649C>T (p.Arg217Cys) or c.650G>A (p.Arg217His) in SLC25A24

evidence.reference_title: SLC25A24 / Fontaine progeroid syndrome (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: SLC25A24 | HGNC:20662 | Fontaine progeroid syndrome | MONDO:0012853 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Heterozygous de novo missense variants at Arg217 alter the SLC25A24 mitochondrial ATP-Mg/phosphate carrier and disrupt its transport dynamics.

evidence.reference_title: De Novo Mutations in SLC25A24 Cause a Craniosynostosis Syndrome with Hypertrichosis, Progeroid Appearance, and Mitochondrial Dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: SLC25A24 encodes a mitochondrial inner membrane ATP-Mg/Pi carrier.

evidence.reference_title: De Novo Mutations in SLC25A24 Cause a Disorder Characterized by Early Aging, Bone Dysplasia, Characteristic Face, and Early Demise.
evidence.snippet: SLC25A24 allows an electro-neutral and reversible exchange of ATP-Mg and phosphate between the cytosol and mitochondria

evidence.reference_title: De Novo Mutations in SLC25A24 Cause a Disorder Characterized by Early Aging, Bone Dysplasia, Characteristic Face, and Early Demise.
evidence.snippet: p.Arg217Cys and p.Arg217His narrow the substrate cavity of the protein and disrupt transporter dynamics.
